summary: Hotfix testing for v5 invalidateQueries fix on payment/coach-rent revert. Test 1 (payment revert auto-refresh) shows a CRITICAL issue: clicking 'Confirmer le retour en impayé' fires the POST /payments/{id}/revert-to-unpaid (HTTP 200) but the dialog stays OPEN with the confirm button stuck in loading ('...') state for >4 seconds, and the row UI does NOT auto-refresh in the required 2-3s window. Tests 2-4 could not be reliably executed in the same run due to environment instability (table loading state when programmatically setting month picker, club selector persisting Lausanne across sessions). Code review confirms the v5 syntax fix IS correctly applied in PaymentsPage.js, CoachesPage.js, MembersPage.js. The user-facing symptom (no UI refresh in ~2s) appears to PERSIST despite the fix, likely because the chained `await Promise.all([refetchQueries(['payments']), refetchQueries(['payments','late']), refetchQueries(['payments','upcoming'])])` is awaited inside onSuccess and blocks both dialog close and UI update until all 3 lists have refetched (with 73+ paid payments per query, this can exceed 4s).

tests_executed.Test 1 — Payment revert auto-refresh: FAIL — Reproduced on payment 957faaf8 (Laura Ngo-Fontaine, Versoix, 90 CHF). POST revert-to-unpaid succeeded (single API call observed). At t+4s: revert-payment-{id} button still present, mark-paid-{id} not present, dialog still open with confirm button in '...' loading state. The UI does NOT auto-refresh within the 2-3 second user requirement. (Side-effect from iter74 already had this payment as paid; status was reverted to 'late' during this test and was NOT restored back to paid because the dialog never reached its post-success state in test window.)
tests_executed.Test 2 — Coach rent revert auto-refresh: NOT EXECUTED — Test 1 failure indicated likely same root cause; prioritized investigating Test 1.
tests_executed.Test 3 — Archives non-régression: NOT EXECUTED — was lower priority than Test 1.
tests_executed.Test 4 — Members archive non-régression: NOT EXECUTED — was lower priority than Test 1.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking revert-payment-confirm-btn, dialog stays open with loading spinner for >4s and the table row badge/buttons do not refresh within the required 2-3s window. POST /revert-to-unpaid is fired (200 OK) but onSuccess awaits 3 sequential refetchQueries that block the UI update. User still effectively needs to wait noticeably long (or close the dialog manually).

integration_issues.issue: await Promise.all([refetchQueries({queryKey:['payments']}), refetchQueries({queryKey:['payments','late']}), refetchQueries({queryKey:['payments','upcoming']})]) inside onSuccess delays both dialog close and visible UI update beyond user's 2-3s requirement. Recommend: 1) Use invalidateQueries (non-awaited) for ['payments'] which will trigger active refetch — the v5 {queryKey} syntax is already applied elsewhere in the same file. 2) OR close the dialog immediately in onMutate/onSuccess BEFORE awaiting refetches. 3) OR use refetchQueries without await (fire-and-forget) so React Query updates rendered list as soon as data arrives but doesn't block dialog close.

action_items: HIGH: Refactor revertPaymentMutation onSuccess in /app/frontend/src/pages/PaymentsPage.js so that dialog close and UI refresh happen within ≤2s. Either remove the `await Promise.all(...)` (use invalidateQueries with v5 syntax {queryKey:['payments']} — same hook, different return), or close the dialog (setRevertDialog(null) etc.) BEFORE awaiting, or trigger the refetches in parallel without awaiting them.

MEDIUM: Apply the same review to revertCoachRentMutation in /app/frontend/src/pages/CoachesPage.js (lines ~140-145) which also awaits refetchQueries({queryKey:['coaches']}, type:'active'). Likely same UX delay, especially given the previously reported parent edit-modal-stays-open issue in iter74.

MEDIUM: Restore payment 957faaf8-a953-4ba8-a31e-6bef50bc0c36 (Laura Ngo-Fontaine, Versoix oct 2025) from 'late' back to 'paid' — was not restored automatically because cleanup branch never executed (dialog hung).

TESTING ENV: The mai 2026 default month + persistent club selection across login sessions made it tricky to find paid payments. The 73 paid payments cited in the brief live in Versoix oct 2025. Future testing agents should programmatically navigate to that view at start, OR seed a fresh paid payment for the test.
critical_code_review_comments: PaymentsPage.js lines 188-193: revertPaymentMutation.onSuccess awaits 3 refetchQueries serially via Promise.all. This BLOCKS the dialog from closing (since useMutation.isPending stays true until onSuccess resolves), causing the exact symptom the user reported. Recommended pattern: fire invalidateQueries (non-awaited) and let TanStack handle the active refetch; the dialog/UI will update reactively as soon as the new data lands.

CoachesPage.js line 143: same pattern — `await queryClient.refetchQueries({queryKey:['coaches'], type:'active'})` will keep the revert-coach-rent dialog in pending state. Combined with the existing 'parent edit modal stays open' issue noted in iter74, this likely delivers the same poor UX.

Positive: All other invalidateQueries calls in PaymentsPage.js, CoachesPage.js, MembersPage.js have been correctly migrated to the v5 object syntax `{queryKey: [...]}`. The fix scope is well-contained.

Suggestion: A simple pattern that satisfies both 'fix v5 deprecation' AND 'fast UI refresh' is: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['payments']})` (no await) — this triggers active refetch via the queryClient and returns synchronously, allowing the mutation to settle and the dialog to close immediately. The list will rerender once the GET resolves (typically <1s).

success_rate.frontend: 0/4 tests passed (1 critical fail, 3 not run)

seed_data_creation: Payment 957faaf8 (Laura Ngo-Fontaine, Versoix, 90 CHF, oct 2025 view) was already 'late' from iter74 testing residue — actually iter74 reverted it from paid→late. In iter75 testing, my agent ran a revert on this same payment (was paid in mai 2026 view due to date coincidence?) — the POST succeeded but cleanup did not run. State after iter75: still likely 'late' (or possibly nothing changed because backend may have rejected reverting an already-late payment — needs E1 to verify in DB).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The hotfix did NOT fully solve the user-reported issue. While the v5 object syntax migration is correct, the awaited Promise.all pattern keeps the mutation in pending state for >4s with 73-payment lists, blocking dialog close and UI refresh. E1 needs to either (a) drop the await, (b) use invalidateQueries instead of refetchQueries, or (c) close the dialog before awaiting refetches. After E1's fix, retest by: 1) login as antoine.paucod, 2) navigate to /payments, 3) switch club to Transform Versoix, 4) set month to oct 2025 (where 73 paid exist), 5) click any revert-payment-* button, confirm WITHOUT email, and assert that within 2s the dialog closes AND the row's revert-payment-* btn is replaced by mark-paid-* btn. Also retest Test 2 (coach rent revert) and Tests 3-4 (archives non-régression) which were not run.
rca of the issue: The original bug was caused by TanStack Query v5 dropping the positional invalidateQueries(['key']) API. E1's fix correctly migrated all calls to {queryKey:[...]} object syntax, AND additionally introduced an `await Promise.all([refetchQueries(...)])` chain inside revertPaymentMutation.onSuccess specifically. This second change inadvertently introduced a new symptom: useMutation does not transition out of `isPending` until onSuccess resolves; refetching 3 lists serially with 73+ items each takes longer than the user's 2-3s tolerance window. The dialog (which uses isPending to disable the confirm button) thus stays open with the spinner. Mitigation: drop `await` (let refetches happen async), OR replace refetchQueries with invalidateQueries (returns immediately with active refetch behavior identical to refetchQueries({type:'active'})), OR close the dialog manually before awaiting.
